name: METFORAGING metformin epigenetic-age pilot trial
summary: A 96-week double-blind randomized placebo-controlled pilot trial testing 850 mg metformin twice daily against epigenetic-age biomarkers in non-diabetic older people living with controlled HIV.
population: Non-diabetic people living with HIV aged 50 years or older with virologic suppression and CD4+ T-cell counts above 500 cells/uL

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item; the EU register still listed the trial as ongoing with no registry result when checked on 2026-06-14.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for AMPK and Metabolic Modulators because they can show whether the intervention changes biomarkers and safety in Non-diabetic people living with HIV aged 50 years or older with virologic suppression and CD4+ T-cell counts above 500 cells/uL. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
